MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ron Archer of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Christopher Strunk of Gordon & Rees (986.5900) for defendant and moving party

Argued. The Court denied plaintiff's request for additional testimony and adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery responses and the declaration of Wrobel. Defendant's identification of witnesses was devoid of any facts as to what the witnesses knew or contact information. Here plaintiff had contact information for at least one witness, Mr. Speck. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact. Defendant's objections to the Declarations of Speck and Ay are sustained based on Thoren v. Johnston & Washer (1972) 29 Cal. App. 3d 270 as to the Speck Declaration. Defendant has established the underlying facts for the Court to find the failure to disclose what Mr. Speck knew and where he resided was on bad faith. Further, Mr. Speck's subsequent deposition testimony demonstrates that he lacks the knowledge to establish that Mr. Carolan was exposed to asbestos from defendant's products. Ay's testimony is based on Speck's declaration and may not be used to establish the existence of the control fact at issue, contact with a Leslie product containing asbestos. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Michelle Clowser of Brydon Hugo & Parker (808.0300) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on the matter. Ms. Clowser stated her firm represents the defendant but she is not aware of this particular case. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied.
